states.mouseMapping: Used when the pointer is outside the visible sprite and no transient interaction is active.
authoringNotes: Keep motion subtle; this is the reduced-motion safe row.

states.mouseMapping: Used while dragging the desktop pet mostly to the right.
authoringNotes: Use body, limb, and prop movement only; no speed lines, dust, shadows, or trails.

states.mouseMapping: Used while dragging the desktop pet mostly to the left.
authoringNotes: Mirror running-right only when markings, props, handedness, and direction semantics stay correct.

states.mouseMapping: Used after a short hold without dragging.
authoringNotes: Show the gesture through paw pose only.
Do not draw wave marks, arcs, symbols, sparkles, or detached effects.

states.mouseMapping: Used while dragging the desktop pet mostly upward.
authoringNotes: Show anticipation, lift, peak, descent, and settle.
Show vertical motion through pose and body position only; no shadows, dust, landing marks, bounce pads, or motion marks.

states.mouseMapping: Used as a transient reaction after a simple click.
authoringNotes: Use slumped pose, sad eyes, drooping limbs, or lower body position.
Tears, smoke, or stars must be attached to the pet silhouette.

states.mouseMapping: Used while the pointer is over visible pet pixels and no press or drag is active.
authoringNotes: Keep it ready and readable, distinct from idle but not a new identity.

states.mouseMapping: Reserved compatible row; the current mouse runtime does not actively trigger it.
authoringNotes: Spare neutral drag or in-place movement loop.
Keep valid even if the current mouse runtime does not actively trigger it.

states.mouseMapping: Used while dragging the desktop pet mostly downward.
authoringNotes: Use lean, blink, narrowed eyes, head tilt, or paw position.
Avoid papers, code, UI, punctuation, or new props.
